records.ety: [F. multiplier, L. multiplicare, fr. multiplex manifold. See Multitude, Complex.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Multiplied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Multiplying (?).]

defs: To increase in number; to make more numerous; to add quantity to.
quotes: Impunity will multiply motives to disobedience.

defs: To add (any given number or quantity) to itself a certain number of times; to find the product of by multiplication; thus 7 multiplied by 8 produces the number 56; to multiply two numbers. See the Note under Multiplication.
To increase (the amount of gold or silver) by the arts of alchemy.
To become greater in number; to become numerous.
quotes: When men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them.

defs: To increase in extent and influence; to spread.
quotes: The word of God grew and multiplied.

defs: To increase amount of gold or silver by the arts of alchemy.
